channel.title: kopiro
channel.description: SWE at Spotify. I like RJ45 cables.

item.title: Investing 101 in Sweden
item.description: A simple guide to start investing in Sweden as a long-term, passive investor. No finance degree needed.

item.title: My Sage Barista espresso machine is now smart
item.description: The goal is simple: having my Sage Barista already warm when I wake up.

item.title: Make the IKEA VARMBLIXT Donut lamp smart with Zigbee
item.description: I recently bought the IKEA VARMBLIXT donut lamp, but to my disappointment this light is not smart and not dimmable.

item.title: How to use different Git identities per directory
item.description: If you're like me, you have a personal email for your side projects and a work email for... well, work. And if you've ever accidentally committed to your com...

item.title: Claude Code while you drink an espresso
item.description: We have all been there: you are on a 4 hour session with Claude Code and you really need a break; it's in the middle of a big refactoring and you desperately...

item.title: How to use your LG remote to change OLED brightness
item.description: This tutorial is for everyone who wants to use their LG remote to change the brightness of their OLED TV.

item.title: Convert a batch of images into Ghibli style using ChatGPT and macOS Automator
item.description: If you love the warm, dreamy visuals of Studio Ghibli films, here's a powerful automation trick: an AppleScript automation that lets you select multiple imag...

item.title: How to correctly publish your Mac apps outside of the App Store
item.description: Distributing Mac apps outside of the App Store requires more than just building and zipping your .app.

item.title: Using C89 on a '80s Macintosh SE to solve Advent of Code 2023
item.description: For this year AdventOfCode, I want to try to solve some problems using an old Macintosh SE I have lying around and the ThinkC compiler.

item.title: Hey Siri, make coffee!
item.description: “my wife was tired of bringing me coffee in bed every morning. But I am too lazy (especially in the morning) to actually get up and make it. Therefore, I aut...

item.title: How I built the SiriWaveJS library: a look at the math and the code
item.description: It was 4 years ago when I had the idea to replicate the Apple® Siri wave-form (introduced with the iPhone 4S) in the browser using pure Javascript.

item.title: How to easily run any Linux tool on any machine
item.description: Have you ever encountered a situation like the ones below?

item.title: The hardware behind Otto: a monkey plush which became my vocal assistant
item.description: Otto is a monkey plush that we found in a highway store during a trip with my girlfriend in February 2017.

item.title: How to install Kali on a USB stick with pure EFI boot on a Mac (and with virtualization via USB on Virtualbox)
item.description: This tutorial is for everyone who wants a USB stick with a full Kali installation to use with your Mac(s). This is not intended to perform a Live Kali instal...

item.title: What I learned hacking the Facebook Messenger Soccer game
item.description: Recently, during the last European Football Championship, Facebook introduced a little game in the Messenger app that makes you lose hours and hours despite...

item.title: Kata resolution: Next bigger number with the same digits
item.description: I would like to share with you my solution of a Kata on CodeWars.
